title: arachnorchis longicauda subsp. crassa
intro: Arachnorchis longicauda subsp. crassa, also known as the crassum spider orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to Western Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly as a source of the compound epimedophilone, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Arachnorchis longicauda subsp. crassa include its potential to treat various infections and skin conditions, as well as its use in traditional medicine to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made through extraction and drying, with the resulting products often used in topical applications and traditional remedies.
